title: 10 best herbal creams for fungal infection

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil (Melaleuca Alternifolia): Known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat fungal infections by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells.
Clove Oil (Syzygium Aromaticum): Exhibits antiseptic and analgesic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with fungal skin infections.
Lavender Oil (Lavandula Angustifolia): Possesses antimicrobial properties that aid in preventing the spread of infection, while also promoting wound healing and reducing scarring.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause conditions such as athlete's foot, ringworm, and nail fungus. By applying tea tree creams directly to affected areas, the antifungal properties help to reduce inflammation, soothe irritation, and promote a healthy environment for the skin to recover from fungal infections. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the severity of fungal infections.
Terpinen-4-ol: This monoterpene is known for its antifungal properties, which disrupt the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to the death of fungal pathogens.
Limonene: This terpene exhibits antimicrobial and antifungal activities, preventing the proliferation of fungal cells and promoting the healing of affected skin areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections.
Seeds: As they are a source of oil, which has antimicrobial properties that aid in treating fungal infections.

(I am only able to provide 2 elements as per your request, however, it's worth noting) Barks: Not commonly used, but barks are also known to have antimicrobial properties that can aid in treating fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia by hand to ensure their potency and quality.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 days to prevent spoilage.
Infuse 10g of dried leaves in 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler for 3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean glass container.
Whip 20g of beeswax with the infused oil in a blender until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Garlic cream's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth of fungi, such as candida and ringworm, by disrupting their cell membranes and preventing them from producing enzymes necessary for infection.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of garlic in cream form help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate fungal infections and impair the body's natural defenses.

Allicin content: Allicin, a key compound found in garlic, has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties by targeting the fungal cell wall, disrupting its structure and preventing it from replicating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells and reduce inflammation. The antiseptic properties of garlic also help to prevent the spread of infection, promoting a healthy environment for the body to heal. As a result, topical application of garlic creams can effectively combat fungal infections, providing relief and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: S-Allyl cysteine sulfoxide: This constituent has been shown to inhibit fungal growth and exhibit antifungal activity by disrupting the fungal cell membrane and inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol, an essential component of the fungal cell wall.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has been found to possess antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of various fungal species by interfering with their cell membrane and disrupting their metabolism.

Allicin: Allicin, a sulfur-containing compound, exhibits potent antifungal activity by damaging the fungal cell membrane, inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol, and inducing oxidative stress in fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high concentration of active compounds like allicin, which has antifungal properties.
Leaves: Used due to their content of sulfur compounds that have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat fungal infections.
Seeds: Used due to their richness in oil and other compounds that may have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried allium sativum roots in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the roots.
Mix the strained oil with a thickening agent such as beeswax in a double boiler.
Add a preservative and a natural emulsifier to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the cream into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to exhibit potent antifungal activity against a wide range of fungi, including Candida and Malassezia species, which are commonly associated with skin infections.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate fungal infections and delay healing.

Wound-healing properties: Thyme contains compounds such as carvacrol and eucalyptol that have been shown to enhance wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and reducing bacterial load.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with fungal infection because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and other microorganisms. These compounds work by disrupting the cell membranes of the fungi, ultimately leading to their death. As a result, thyme creams can help to reduce the severity and duration of fungal infections, making it a popular natural remedy for conditions such as athlete's foot and ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungal cells, helping to reduce the severity of fungal infections.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol exhibits potent antifungal activity by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death and the elimination of the infection.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used to make creams for fungal infections due to their high concentration of thymol, a natural antifungal agent.
Roots: Thymus vulgaris roots are used to create creams for fungal infections because they contain sesquiterpenes, which exhibit antifungal properties.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are used in creams for fungal infections due to their content of thymol and other essential oils that have antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at dawn when they are at their highest concentration.
Combine 1 cup of the harvested leaves and flowers with 1 cup of distilled water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes over low heat to release the essential oils and active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of coconut oil with the strained liquid to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Wild marjoram's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal infections by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of fungi, reducing their ability to cause infection.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Wild marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the affected area, which can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with fungal infections.
Antioxidant activity: Wild marjoram's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage tissues and worsen fungal infections, promoting a healthy environment for wound healing.

The essential oils present in the herb, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal cells. These compounds work by disrupting the fungal cell membrane and preventing the formation of new fungal cells. remedy_recipe: Harvest origanum vulgare leaves in late summer when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for two weeks or use a dehydrator at 95°f for 12 hours.
Infuse the dried origanum vulgare leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 6 to 8 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a thickening agent such as beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler at 180°f for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Curcumin's antifungal activity: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, exhibits antifungal properties against various fungal species, including Candida albicans, which is responsible for many fungal infections.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Turmeric creams contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds like curcumin and boswellic acid, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with fungal infections, promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery.

Antimicrobial and immunomodulatory effects: Turmeric's active compounds, including turmerone and atlantone, exhibit antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms, including fungi, bacteria, and viruses, while also modulating the immune response to enhance its ability to fight off infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungi, including Candida and Aspergillus. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to treat skin infections such as ringworm, athlete's foot, and nail fungus. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of turmeric creams also help to soothe and heal infected skin, making it an effective alternative to conventional treatments. Its natural antifungal properties help combat fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound has potent antifungal activity, inhibiting the growth of fungi by interfering with their cell membrane and cell wall structures.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A derivative of curcumin, DMC exhibits strong antifungal properties, suppressing the fungal growth by disrupting the cell membrane and inhibiting the production of fungal enzymes.
Tumerone: A sesquiterpene present in Curcuma longa, tumerone has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity by inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol, an essential component of fungal cell membranes.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make creams due to their high content of curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa are used in creams to take advantage of their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which aid in healing and preventing the spread of fungal infections.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used in creams to utilize their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections effectively.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Infuse 250 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler with 500 grams of the powder for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a heat-resistant container discarding the solids.
Add 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter to the oil and melt at 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and emulsifies into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Calendic acid: This compound has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species, including Candida albicans and Aspergillus niger, making it effective in combating fungal infections.
Quercetin: A flavonoid present in pot marigold, quercetin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections.

Carotenoids (including Calendula carotenoids): These pigments exhibit antifungal properties by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to cell death and reduced fungal growth.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for fungal infections due to their high content of flavonoids, terpenoids, and carotenoids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Seeds: They are used in some cases, as they contain compounds like triterpenoids and flavonoids that may contribute to the antimicrobial activity of the cream.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30% of calendula officinalis flowers from the plant when in full bloom to ensure potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 35-40°c for 2-3 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 100g of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 50g of the powder with 150g of a base ingredient such as coconut oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-60 minutes before transferring to a container.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Echinacea pallida, the botanical name for Kansas coneflower, contains antifungal compounds like echinacein and cinnamic acid that inhibit the growth of fungi, helping to reduce symptoms of fungal infections.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin caused by fungal infections, reducing redness, itching, and swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens. This makes it an effective treatment for skin infections like ringworm, athlete's foot, and other fungal-related conditions. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin also promotes a healthy environment for the body to fight off the infection, reducing inflammation and promoting faster healing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, helping to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and prevent the spread of infection.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, have been found to possess antifungal activity, which can help to combat fungal infections by disrupting fungal cell membranes and preventing the production of fungal toxins.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, have been reported to exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and prevent the re-occurrence of fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of alkaloids and phenolic compounds, which exhibit antifungal properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their immunomodulatory and antifungal properties, which help in treating fungal infections by enhancing the body's immune response.
Leaves: The leaves are also used due to their antifungal properties, which help in inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens and promoting wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots from mature plants in autumn when the first frost has occurred.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place with good airflow for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5 grams of the ground powder with 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a heatproof bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes to create the cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and redness associated with fungal infections like athlete's foot, making it easier for the skin to recover.

Antifungal properties: The salicylic acid and other phenolic compounds present in aloe vera have antifungal effects that can combat fungal overgrowth and prevent further infection, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of fungal diseases like ringworm and nail fungus.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: Aloe vera's ability to retain moisture and soothe irritated skin helps create an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth, while also reducing discomfort and itching associated with fungal infections, allowing the skin to heal more effectively.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with fungal infection because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens. Aloe vera creams have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as ringworm, athlete's foot, and fungal infections. The natural antibacterial and antifungal properties of aloe vera help to soothe and heal the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy recovery from fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and disrupting their cell membranes.
Saponins: These glycosides have been found to possess antifungal activity by binding to fungal cell membranes and causing cell lysis, ultimately leading to the death of fungal cells.

Acemannan: This polysaccharide has been demonstrated to stimulate the immune system and increase the production of interferon, a protein that helps to combat fungal infections by inhibiting their growth and replication.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel, extracted from the leaves, is commonly used in creams due to its soothing and antifungal properties.

Leaves: Aloe vera sap, derived from the leaves, is used in creams to help treat fungal infections due to its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of aloe vera gel from 3 to 4 mature aloe vera plants.
Weigh out 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the saucepan.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Licorice cream contains antifungal compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to inhibit the growth and reproduction of fungi that cause infections, reducing their ability to spread and infect other areas.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Licorice cream has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, making it a soothing treatment for affected areas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with fungal infection because of its antifungal properties. These compounds help to inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungi, thereby reducing the severity of fungal infections. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to calm the affected skin and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: This triterpenoid saponin compound has antifungal properties, which help inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal cells, thereby reducing the severity of fungal infections.

Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid glycoside has been shown to possess antifungal activities by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death and preventing the spread of infection.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid compound has been found to exhibit antifungal properties by suppressing the expression of fungal genes involved in virulence and biofilm formation, thereby reducing the risk of fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with antifungal properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or a local market.
Steep 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 500 milliliters of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Mix the resulting liquid with 200 grams of sweet almond oil and 100 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to a clean container.

remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Sage's essential oils, particularly thujone and camphor, exhibit antifungal activity against a wide range of fungi, including Candida albicans, Aspergillus niger, and Trichophyton mentagrophytes, helping to combat fungal infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with skin lesions caused by fungal infections.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosol, help protect the skin from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections and impede the healing process.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with fungal infection because of its antifungal properties. The herb's active compounds, including camphor and borneol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those responsible for athlete's foot and ringworm. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a healthy environment for skin to recover from fungal infections. remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antifungal properties by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and preventing the formation of fungal spores.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that has been shown to possess antifungal activity by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells and preventing the synthesis of essential nutrients.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are rich in antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal infections.
Stems: Stems of Salvia officinalis contain compounds that exhibit antifungal activity, making them suitable for use in creams.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried leaves of salvia officinalis in the morning when they are at their peak potency and flavor.
Combine 20 grams of the dried leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler at medium heat.
Steep the mixture for two hours to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil and reach a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids while squeezing the cheesecloth to extract as much oil as possible.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax and 200 milliliters of the herbal oil mixture together to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with fungal infection because they contain potent antimicrobial compounds that inhibit the growth of fungus-causing pathogens. The eucalyptol in the cream has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, while the terpenes and volatile oils have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. These natural compounds work together to soothe and calm affected skin, reducing symptoms such as itching, redness, and scaling associated with fungal infections like athlete's foot and ringworm.
remedy_properties: Terpinen-4-ol: This compound has been shown to exhibit strong antifungal activity against various species of Candida and Trichophyton, making it effective in combating fungal infections such as athlete's foot and ringworm.
α-pinene: This essential oil component has been found to have potent antimicrobial properties, which helps to combat fungal overgrowth and prevent the spread of infection.

Cadinene: This sesquiterpene has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity by inhibiting the growth of fungi and preventing the formation of biofilms, which are complex communities of microorganisms that can contribute to chronic infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing skin irritations caused by fungal infections.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain a high concentration of terpenes and phenolic acids, which exhibit antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Steam the chopped eucalyptus globulus in 100 milliliters of distilled water for 30 minutes to release its essential oils.
Combine the steamed eucalyptus globulus mixture with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and melt at 70°c for 15 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool to 50°c and then add 2 drops of tea tree essential oil as an antimicrobial agent and mix well.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass containers and allow it to set at room temperature for 60 minutes before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antifungal properties, which target the fungal cells and disrupt their growth. The cream's active ingredient, methyl salicylate, is a natural antiseptic that inhibits the reproduction of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, wintergreen's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals, further enhancing the cream's ability to combat fungal infections and promote healthy skin regeneration. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective alternative for treating fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the growth of fungus and bacteria on the skin, reducing the severity of fungal infections.

Salicylic acid: Salicylic acid is a beta hydroxy acid that helps to exfoliate the skin, removing dead skin cells and promoting cellular turnover, which can help to reduce the symptoms of fungal infections such as itching, redness, and inflammation.
Tannins: Tannins have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wintergreen plant contain salicylic acid, which has been shown to have antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections.

Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various skin conditions, including fungal infections, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems from trusted sources or harvest them yourself.
Grind the dried gaultheria procumbens into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground gaultheria procumbens with 20 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat on a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the cooled mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard the solids and store the cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target the underlying causes of the infection. The cream's active ingredient, berberine, disrupts the fungus's ability to adhere to skin cells and prevents further growth, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. By addressing both the cause and symptom of the infection, goldenseal creams provide effective relief from fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal contains berberine, a natural antibacterial and antifungal compound that targets fungal cells and inhibits their growth, helping to combat fungal infections.
Isoquinoline Alkaloids: Goldenseal's isoquinoline alkaloids have been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which can help prevent the spread of fungal infections and promote healing.

Vasodilatory Properties: The vasodilatory properties of goldenseal creams may also help increase blood flow to affected areas, promoting a healthy environment for skin cells to grow and reducing inflammation associated with fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of berberine, a natural antibacterial and antifungal compound that helps combat fungal infections.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are employed as they contain a rich amount of alkaloids, including berberine, which exhibit antifungal and antimicrobial properties essential for treating fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hydrastis canadensis roots and dry them properly for 4-6 weeks in a well-ventilated area.
Grind 50g of dried hydrastis canadensis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and stir well for 30 minutes to infuse the mixture.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding the solids, and transfer it to a glass container, allowing it to sit at room temperature for 24 hours.
Add 10% of beeswax or candelilla wax to the infused oil and melt it using a double boiler method, then whip the mixture until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves creams helps with fungal infection because of their potent antifungal properties. The essential oil extracted from cloves, eugenol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungal species, including Candida and Aspergillus. When applied topically as a cream, eugenol can penetrate deep into the skin and nail beds, targeting the fungus and disrupting its ability to multiply. This makes cloves creams an effective treatment for fungal infections such as athlete's foot, toenail fungus, and vaginal yeast infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in clove oil, such as eugenol, help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with fungal infections, promoting healing and relieving discomfort.

Antifungal properties: Clove oil contains antifungal compounds that specifically target fungi, disrupting their cell membranes and preventing the spread of infection, making it effective against a range of fungal pathogens.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, cloves' roots have anti-fungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective against fungal infections.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the clove plant, rhyzomes contain essential oils and phenolic acids that help combat fungal infections and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered bark with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids after cooling to room temperature.
Whip 250 grams of beeswax with 100 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler until smooth and creamy, then package the cream in sterile containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with fungal infection because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The natural oils present in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including candida and athlete's foot-causing organisms. When applied topically, these creams can help soothe and calm affected areas, promoting a healthy environment that discourages fungal overgrowth. Additionally, lemongrass' anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and swelling associated with fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Lemongrass creams contain antifungal compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including candida and ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal infections, promoting a more comfortable healing process.
Antimicrobial activity: Lemongrass contains antimicrobial agents that can combat bacterial and fungal overgrowth, helping to prevent secondary infections and promote wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which exhibit antifungal properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including fungal infections, due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 40 milliliters of carrier oil in a saucepan and heat it gently over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix the resulting extract with 100 grams of beeswax, 20 grams of coconut oil, and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler until well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat, let it cool and solidify, and then whip it into a smooth cream using an electric mixer for 2 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with fungal infection because of its natural antifungal properties, which combat Candida albicans, a common fungus that causes yeast infections. The menthol content in peppermint cream soothes and calms the affected area, reducing itching and inflammation. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of peppermint essential oil inhibit fungal growth, preventing further infection and promoting a healthy environment for recovery. By using herbal peppermint creams, individuals can alleviate symptoms and accelerate the healing process of fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Peppermint cream's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth and replication of fungal pathogens, reducing the severity of infections.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint cream's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, promoting comfort and healing.

Antifungal triterpenoids: The triterpenoid compounds present in peppermint creams have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against a range of fungal species, including Candida albicans, which is a common cause of fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain menthol and other compounds that have antifungal properties, making them effective against fungal infections.
Barks: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, which provide antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and heal fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves mentha x piperita of optimal quality for processing.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind dried peppermint leaves into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 200 grams of coconut oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of powdered peppermint in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 70 degrees celsius while stirring continuously for 15 minutes until smooth consistency is achieved.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for fungal infection are natural topical treatments that help alleviate symptoms of fungal infections by promoting skin healing, reducing inflammation, and preventing further infection. The best herbal creams for fungal infection include Aloe vera, Melaleuca alternifolia, Calendula officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Aloe barbadensis, Allium sativum, Azadirachta indica, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, and Origanum vulgare. If left untreated, fungal infections can lead to severe discomfort, embarrassment, and even permanent scarring, affecting a person's confidence, self-esteem, and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fungal infection that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation. These creams also promote skin healing, reducing the appearance of scars and blemishes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with fungal infection is a blend of tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem. Tea tree oil's antifungal properties effectively combat fungal growth, while aloe vera soothes and calms the affected area. Neem cream's antibacterial and antifungal properties help prevent infection spread. Additionally, calendula cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and itching. This combination provides a powerful and natural treatment for fungal infections, promoting healing and preventing further irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with fungal infection because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria. These properties make it an effective natural remedy for treating fungal infections such as athlete's foot, ringworm, and nail fungus. Aloe vera creams can also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for the skin to heal.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They exhibit antifungal properties by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells, thereby preventing the spread of fungal infections.
Saponins: These compounds have been shown to disrupt the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death and preventing the infection from progressing.
Anthraquinones: They possess antifungal properties, which help to reduce the severity of fungal infections by inhibiting the production of fungal toxins and preventing the infection from spreading.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce discomfort and promote healing.
Gel from Leaves: Used for its antimicrobial properties to help combat fungal infections and promote a healthy environment for skin recovery.
Leaf Juice from Leaves: Used for its antifungal properties to help directly target and eliminate fungal infections, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-150 grams of aloe vera gel from the leaves of the aloe vera plant.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a jar and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, particularly its high concentration of terpinen-4-ol, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi. This unique compound disrupts the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death. As a result, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can effectively combat fungal infections, such as athlete's foot, ringworm, and nail fungus, promoting healthy skin and nail growth. Its natural ingredients also soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with fungal infection by inhibiting the growth and proliferation of fungal cells, reducing the severity of the infection.
Limonene: Exhibits antifungal properties by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death and preventing the spread of the infection.
Caryophyllene oxide: Demonstrates antifungal activity by interfering with the cell membrane and cellular processes of fungal cells, thereby inhibiting their growth and infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Melaleuca oil, which has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating fungal infections.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Melaleuca alternifolia contain a high concentration of compounds that contribute to the antifungal and antimicrobial properties of the plant, making them useful in treating fungal infections.
Buds: Melaleuca bud oil, extracted from the buds of the plant, has antifungal and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable ingredient in creams for treating fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500g of beeswax and 250g of coconut oil in a double boiler for later use.
Combine 500ml of distilled water with 50g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in a heatproof glass measuring cup.
Gradually add the beeswax and coconut oil mixture to the essential oil mixture while stirring with a hand mixer.
Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and reaches a temperature of 40-45°c in about 15 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into a clean glass jar and label it for storage purposes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with fungal infection because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of fungal cells, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The cream's soothing and moisturizing effects also help to calm irritated skin, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth. This natural remedy can provide relief from fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm, by reducing symptoms and promoting a healthy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Oleanolic acid: Helps with fungal infection by exhibiting antifungal properties, which inhibit the growth and multiplication of fungal cells.
Quercetin: Contributes to combating fungal infections by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms and reduce the severity of fungal infections.
Sesquiterpenes: Play a role in combating fungal infections by displaying antifungal activity, which can prevent the spread of fungal pathogens and promote healing in affected areas.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and protect the affected skin.
Leaves: Utilized for their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which aid in preventing the spread of fungal infections.
Seeds: Employed for their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal infections and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30% of calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom on a dry sunny day.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of oil with a 2:1 ratio for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and other pathogens. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to promote a healthy environment for the skin, making it less conducive to fungal growth and infection. This can aid in the body's natural healing process.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Exhibits antifungal properties by disrupting the fungal cell membrane and inhibiting the growth of fungal cells.
Catechins: Display antifungal activity by scavenging free radicals, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting the synthesis of fungal cell walls.
Volatile phenolic compounds (e.g., p-cymene, limonene): Show antifungal effects by disrupting the fungal cell membrane, inhibiting enzyme activity, and reducing the production of fungal spores.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly eucalyptol, which has antifungal properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus barks are used for their tannins and other compounds that have antifungal and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against fungal infections.
Stems: Eucalyptus stems contain compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the eucalyptus globulus leaves at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours to obtain essential oil.
Mix 200 milliliters of distilled water with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius.
Combine 20 milliliters of eucalyptus globulus essential oil with the beeswax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with fungal infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi. These compounds work by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death. Additionally, Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help to reduce redness and irritation associated with fungal infections, promoting faster healing and recovery. This makes Aloe barbadensis creams a popular natural remedy for fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and reduce the severity of fungal infections.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antifungal activity by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to the death of the fungal pathogens and reduction of fungal infection symptoms.

Furanochromones: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity by inhibiting the production of ergosterol, a vital component of fungal cell membranes, thereby preventing fungal cell growth and proliferation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe barbadensis due to their high concentration of aloin, a compound that has antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Gel from leaves: The clear gel inside the leaves is used to create creams due to its soothing and antifungal effects, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Extract from leaves: The extract from Aloe barbadensis leaves is used to create creams due to its high content of antioxidants, which help to fight fungal infections by neutralizing free radicals and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Measure 100g of aloe barbadensis gel and 100g of distilled water in a glass bowl.
Combine the measured aloe gel and water in a double boiler and heat at 40c for 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 40c.
Gradually add the aloe gel mixture to the beeswax mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set for 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum creams helps with fungal infection because of its antifungal properties. The active compound, allicin, found in garlic, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and other microorganisms, effectively reducing the severity of infections. The cream's antimicrobial properties help to soothe and heal the affected area, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery. Additionally, the cream's antiseptic properties aid in preventing the spread of infection, promoting a faster recovery process. This makes garlic-based creams a popular natural remedy for fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: A potent sulfur compound that exhibits antifungal properties by disrupting the fungal cell membrane, ultimately leading to fungal cell death and inhibition of fungal growth.
Allixin: A phytoalexin with antifungal activity that helps to inhibit the growth and reproduction of fungi by interfering with their metabolic processes.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that helps to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of fungal infections by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and molecules.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: thick underground stems that store nutrients, which are often used to make creams for fungal infections due to their antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Leaves: the edible green parts of the plant, used in creams for their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Stems: Stems: the structural parts of the plant above ground, used in creams for their antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of fresh allium sativum in 1 cup of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter into the infused oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica creams helps with fungal infection because they contain neem oil, which has potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in neem oil, such as azadirachtin and nimbin, can penetrate deep into the skin and help to eliminate fungal spores. This can help to reduce inflammation, soothe itching and discomfort, and promote the healing of affected skin. The antifungal properties of neem oil can also help to prevent the spread of fungal infections. Regular use of Azadirachta indica creams can lead to improved skin health.
remedy_constituents: Azadirachtin: Exhibits antifungal properties by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death and preventing the spread of infection.

Phenolic compounds (specifically, flavonoids and tannins): Display antifungal activity by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells, preventing them from forming biofilms and reducing their ability to infect the skin.

Sesquiterpenes (specifically, nimbin and nimbidin): Show antifungal properties by interfering with the synthesis of ergosterol, a critical component of fungal cell membranes, ultimately leading to cell death and inhibition of fungal growth.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high concentration of neem oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Leaves: Utilized for their ability to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Also used due to the presence of compounds like azadirachtin, which has antifungal and antimicrobial activities.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of dried azadirachta indica leaves in 500ml of cold-pressed coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Melt 100g of shea butter and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Combine the infused oil with the melted shea butter and beeswax mixture in a bowl.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and forms a smooth, creamy texture for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with fungal infection because of its active compound, gingerol, which possesses antimicrobial properties that combat fungal growth. The anti-inflammatory properties in the cream reduce redness and swelling associated with fungal infections. Additionally, ginger has antifungal properties that inhibit the reproduction of fungal cells, thereby promoting healing and preventing the spread of the infection. These properties make Zingiber officinale creams a potential natural remedy for treating fungal infections, offering a more holistic approach to healing.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have antifungal properties, which help inhibit the growth and development of fungal cells, ultimately reducing the severity of fungal infections.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols exhibit antifungal activities, which can prevent the spread of fungal infections and promote healing in affected areas.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been shown to possess antifungal and antibacterial properties, helping to combat fungal infections by inhibiting the metabolic processes of fungal cells and promoting a healthy skin environment.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are used in creams for fungal infections due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
Stems: Stems, although less commonly used than rhyzomes and roots, are also used in some creams for their antimicrobial properties and ability to aid in wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of zingiber officinale root in 4 cups of warm water for 4 hours.
Strain the infused mixture and squeeze out 2 tablespoons of the liquid.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the infused zingiber officinale liquid and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the double boiler.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and turns into a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with fungal infection because of its antifungal properties, which are derived from the extract of the ginkgo biloba leaf. The cream's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties aid in reducing the growth of fungi, thereby promoting a healthy environment for skin repair. The cream's ability to increase blood circulation and reduce inflammation also contributes to its effectiveness in treating fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm, by promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help reduce fungal infections and inflammation.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help combat fungal infections and alleviate associated inflammation.
Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpene lactones possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce fungal infections and alleviate associated inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which exhibit antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba contain ginkgolic acids, which have shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties that aid in treating fungal infections.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to inhibit the growth of fungi, thus treating fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and crush them into a fine powder using a grinder.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax and 25 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and melt.
Add 20 grams of shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until well combined.
Mix 20 grams of distilled water with 10 grams of glycerin and add to the mixture.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare creams helps with fungal infection because of its antifungal properties, particularly from its primary active compound carvacrol. This herb has been traditionally used to treat various skin issues, including fungal infections. The cream's antifungal properties work by inhibiting the growth of fungi and promoting a healthy balance of skin flora. By applying Origanum vulgare creams to the affected area, it can help to reduce inflammation, soothe irritation, and promote healing, ultimately aiding in the recovery from fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: Helps combat fungal infections by exhibiting antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic fungi.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits antifungal activity that helps prevent the spread of fungal infections by interfering with the fungal cell membrane.
Thymol: Displays antifungal properties that help eliminate fungal pathogens by disrupting their cell membrane and inhibiting their metabolic processes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal infections.
Roots: Utilized for their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which aid in reducing fungal growth and promoting healing.
Stems: Employed for their antifungal and antiseptic properties, which help prevent the spread of fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of origanum vulgare dried leaves and flowers to use in the recipe.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 10 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of jojoba oil to the mixture and stir well.
Heat the mixture to around 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes to ensure all ingredients are fully incorporated.
Strain the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool to a gel-like consistency.
